title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc gives real R matrix, file-backed — genuine ALTREP numeric vector/matrix whose storage lives mmap-ed file instead R heap, powered patched copy fmalloc. actual R object proxy handle lazy file index, dim(), %*%, crossprod(), Ops, subsetting just work — data can larger RAM, compressed disk, mutated place, multiplied pluggable backend. direction project --core, compressed, -place-mutable matrix stack built three runtime-pluggable tiers fmalloc storage: codecs — bytes decode: builtin f32/f16/bf16, lossless floating-point codec (alp), sparse codec mostly-zero data (single-cell counts), quantized GGUF formats via Rgguf — registered codec registry packages can extend; backends — hardware multiplies: matrix products dispatch dgemm selectable backend (default R’s BLAS; downstream packages can register GPU --core-aware kernel); fmalloc — lives: file-backed, --core, persistent, mutable place. Contrast neighbours: lazy file index (vroom) proxy class (bigmemory, bigstatsr FBM, houba) — real ALTREP object computes compresses core. Earlier prototypes explored R’s custom allocator / Rf_allocVector3() path (see Simon Urbanek’s proof--concept). patched fmalloc malloc/free/realloc layer still built installed native consumers use C API link bundled library.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Storage vectors: fmalloc-backed ALTREP vectors logical, integer, numeric, raw, complex, character, list values; explicit runtime handles persistent / scratch modes; persistent serialization/reopening fixed-width atomic character vectors. Matrix computing: native elementwise Ops, Summary, Math/Math2, matrix reductions; matrix products (%*%, crossprod(), tcrossprod()) call BLAS dgemm finite double operands (managed native loops otherwise), dispatched pluggable backend (fmalloc_matmul_backend()); --core products (fmalloc_matmul_ooc(), fmalloc_crossprod_ooc(), fmalloc_tcrossprod_ooc()) stream column tiles dgemm madvise eviction, operands larger RAM multiply bounded resident set; %*%/crossprod/tcrossprod auto-route RAM-based threshold. Compression (typed tensors): as_fmalloc_tensor() stores matrix codec — lossless "alp" decimal-scaled doubles, "sparse" mostly-zero (single-cell/count) data — compressed tensor still participates panel-streamed matrix products; codecs registry (fmalloc_tensor_codecs()); downstream packages add (Rgguf registers quantized GGUF formats). -place mutation: fmalloc_set(), fmalloc_fill(), fmalloc_add()/sub()/mul()/div() write backing store, bypassing R’s copy--modify — essential payload larger RAM persistent. Native surface: explicit destroy_fmalloc_vector(), -file persistent allocation catalog, runtime/vector introspection, installed C header R_RegisterCCallable() entry points (API version 5), including tensor codec matmul backend registration downstream packages. Known base-fallback boundaries: rowSums()/colSums()/rowMeans()/colMeans() fall back base (warning) input exact 2D matrix dims != 1L; scalar zero-length results Summary, Math, Math2 ordinary R scalars design; matrix products fall back managed loops NA/NaN/Inf logical/integer/complex operands, matching base semantics.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: examples use temporary backing files. snippet runs inside local() .exit() cleanup stays scoped snippet README rendered; required normal interactive use. code, keep runtime handle need call cleanup_fmalloc() finished. shortest form uses init_fmalloc(), opens backing file stores package default runtime. Calls create_fmalloc_vector() can omit runtime argument: multiple backing files explicit lifetime management, prefer runtime handles returned open_fmalloc():
code: library(Rfmalloc) local({   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   init_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc()     unlink(alloc_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10)   v_num <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10)   v_raw <- create_fmalloc_vector("raw", 4)   v_cplx <- create_fmalloc_vector("complex", 4)   v_chr <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3)   v_lst <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2)    v_int[1:3] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   v_num[1:3] <- c(1.1, 2.2, 3.3)   v_raw[] <- as.raw(1:4)   v_cplx[] <- c(1+1i, 2+2i, 3+3i, 4+4i)   v_chr[] <- c("alpha", "beta", NA_character_)    list_child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2)   list_child[] <- 1:2   v_lst[[1]] <- list_child    list(     integer = v_int[1:3],     numeric = v_num[1:3],     raw = v_raw[],     complex = v_cplx[],     character = v_chr[],     list_first = v_lst[[1]][]   ) }) #> $integer #> [1] 1 2 3 #>  #> $numeric #> [1] 1.1 2.2 3.3 #>  #> $raw #> [1] 01 02 03 04 #>  #> $complex #> [1] 1+1i 2+2i 3+3i 4+4i #>  #> $character #> [1] "alpha" "beta"  NA      #>  #> $list_first #> [1] 1 2 local({   handle_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(handle_file)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(handle_file)   }, add = TRUE)    v <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt)   v[1:3] <- 10:12   v[1:3] }) #> [1] 10 11 12

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: example executed building README uses 1B-length fmalloc payload.
code: local({   large_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(large_file, size_gb = 10)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(large_file)   }, add = TRUE)    n <- 10000000L   before_alloc <- nrow(list_fmalloc_allocations(rt))    big_int_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt)   big_int_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt)   big_int_a[1:5] <- 1:5   big_int_b[1:5] <- 6:10    # Elementwise arithmetic stays on fmalloc-managed ALTREP for the full-length result   big_sum <- big_int_a + big_int_b   after_alloc <- nrow(list_fmalloc_allocations(rt))    list(     a_head = big_int_a[1:5],     b_head = big_int_b[1:5],     sum_head = big_sum[1:5],     sum_length = length(big_sum),     sum_managed = inherits(big_sum, "fmalloc") &&       grepl("fmalloc_altrep", capture.output(.Internal(inspect(big_sum)))[[1L]], fixed = TRUE),     allocations_delta = after_alloc - before_alloc   )    # sum(big_sum) is not used here: Summary(S) generics return scalars. }) #> $a_head #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 #>  #> $b_head #> [1]  6  7  8  9 10 #>  #> $sum_head #> [1]  7  9 11 13 15 #>  #> $sum_length #> [1] 10000000 #>  #> $sum_managed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $allocations_delta #> [1] 3

x - y,     mul = x * y,     div = x / y,     idiv = x %/% y,     mod = x %% y,     pow = x ^ 2L,     cmp_eq = x == y,     cmp_lt = x < 20L,     unary_neg = -x,     unary_not = !(x > 20L)   ) }) #> title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: %*%, crossprod(), tcrossprod() fmalloc matrices call BLAS dgemm operands finite doubles, managed native loops otherwise (split base R’s default matrix product uses). Results fmalloc-backed.
code: local({   mm_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(mm_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.1)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(mm_file)   }, add = TRUE)    a <- create_fmalloc_matrix("numeric", nrow = 200, ncol = 80, runtime = rt)   b <- create_fmalloc_matrix("numeric", nrow = 80, ncol = 30, runtime = rt)   set.seed(1)   a[] <- rnorm(200 * 80)   b[] <- rnorm(80 * 30)    z <- a %*% b   list(     dims = dim(z),     fmalloc_backed = is_fmalloc_vector(z),     matches_base = all.equal(as.vector(z), as.vector(matrix(a[], 200) %*% matrix(b[], 80)))   ) }) #> $dims #> [1] 200  30 #>  #> $fmalloc_backed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $matches_base #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: typed tensor fmalloc raw payload foreign element encoding plus dtype/dims tags. genuinely n-dimensional — storage fmalloc_tensor_materialize() handle rank — matrix products, decode payload bounded, block-aligned column panels streamed dgemm (full double representation never materialized), require exactly 2 dimensions. Codecs f64, f32, f16, bf16 builtin; downstream packages register C API (Rgguf adds quantized GGUF formats). builtin "alp" codec compresses decimal-scaled doubles losslessly (Afroozeh et al., ALP; scalar core adapted MIT-licensed zap implementation, see inst/COPYRIGHTS): "sparse" codec stores nonzeros chunk, mostly-zero data single-cell counts — compresses heavily still multiplies panel engine:
code: local({   alp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(alp_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.5)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(alp_file)   }, add = TRUE)    set.seed(2)   # Dosage-like decimal data: 3 decimals in [0, 2].   x <- matrix(round(runif(20000 * 10, 0, 2), 3), nrow = 20000)   ten <- as_fmalloc_tensor(x, runtime = rt)   print(ten)    b <- matrix(rnorm(10 * 4), nrow = 10)   z <- ten %*% b # decoded panel-by-panel inside dgemm    list(     codecs = fmalloc_tensor_codecs(),     compression_ratio = length(unclass(ten)) / (length(x) * 8),     lossless = identical(as.vector(fmalloc_tensor_materialize(ten)[]), as.vector(x)),     product_fmalloc_backed = is_fmalloc_vector(z),     product_matches_base = all.equal(as.vector(z), as.vector(x %*% b))   ) }) #> <fmalloc_tensor alp [20000 x 10], 279720 payload bytes> #> $codecs #> [1] "f64"    "f32"    "f16"    "bf16"   "alp"    "sparse" #>  #> $compression_ratio #> [1] 0.174825 #>  #> $lossless #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $product_fmalloc_backed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $product_matches_base #> [1] TRUE local({   sp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(sp_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.5)   on.exit({ cleanup_fmalloc(rt); unlink(sp_file) }, add = TRUE)    set.seed(3)   # ~10% nonzero small counts (single-cell-like)   X <- matrix(0, 5000, 40)   nz <- sample(length(X), length(X) * 0.1)   X[nz] <- rpois(length(nz), 3) + 1    ten <- as_fmalloc_tensor(X, dtype = "sparse", runtime = rt)   b <- matrix(rnorm(40 * 3), nrow = 40)    list(     dtype = fmalloc_tensor_dtype(ten),     compression_ratio = length(unclass(ten)) / (length(X) * 8),     lossless = identical(as.vector(fmalloc_tensor_materialize(ten)[]), as.vector(X)),     product_matches_base = all.equal(as.vector((ten %*% b)[]), as.vector(X %*% b))   ) }) #> $dtype #> [1] "sparse" #>  #> $compression_ratio #> [1] 0.1522 #>  #> $lossless #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $product_matches_base #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: fmalloc_matmul_ooc(), fmalloc_crossprod_ooc(), fmalloc_tcrossprod_ooc() stream matrix dgemm one contiguous column tile time, releasing tile’s pages madvise(MADV_DONTNEED), matrix larger RAM multiplies resident set bounded tile size rather matrix size. crossprod(X) = X'X covariance/Gram matrix behind PCA, ridge, GWAS. %*%/crossprod()/tcrossprod() fmalloc matrix auto-route operand reaches getOption("Rfmalloc.ooc_threshold_gb") (default half physical RAM). example forces threshold 0 show path small matrix:
code: local({   ooc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ooc_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.5)   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt); unlink(ooc_file)     options(Rfmalloc.ooc_threshold_gb = NULL, Rfmalloc.ooc_tile_mb = NULL)   }, add = TRUE)    set.seed(4)   X <- create_fmalloc_matrix("numeric", nrow = 4000, ncol = 200, runtime = rt)   bx <- matrix(rnorm(4000 * 200), 4000, 200)   X[] <- bx    # explicit out-of-core Gram matrix   G <- fmalloc_crossprod_ooc(X, tile_mb = 8)    # auto-routing: force the threshold so crossprod(X) takes the OOC path   options(Rfmalloc.ooc_threshold_gb = 0, Rfmalloc.ooc_tile_mb = 8)   list(     gram_matches_base = all.equal(as.vector(G[]), as.vector(crossprod(bx))),     gram_is_fmalloc_backed = is_fmalloc_vector(G),     auto_routed_matches = all.equal(as.vector(crossprod(X)[]), as.vector(crossprod(bx)))   ) }) #> $gram_matches_base #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $gram_is_fmalloc_backed #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $auto_routed_matches #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: unshared fmalloc vector, ordinary x[] <- value already writes place ALTREP data pointer (copy — benefit file-backed ALTREP design). copy hurts happens vector shared (y <- x): R duplicates whole payload preserve value semantics, catastrophic larger RAM. fmalloc_set()/ fmalloc_fill() fmalloc_add()/sub()/mul()/div() mutate reference regardless sharing, bindings observe change — deliberately, ever explicit functions, never silent [<-.
code: local({   ip_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ip_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.1)   on.exit({ cleanup_fmalloc(rt); unlink(ip_file) }, add = TRUE)    x <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 6, runtime = rt)   fmalloc_fill(x, 1)          # x[] <- 1, no copy   fmalloc_set(x, c(2, 4), 9)  # x[c(2,4)] <- 9, no copy   fmalloc_add(x, 10)          # x <- x + 10, in place    y <- x                      # no copy on assignment   fmalloc_mul(x, 2)           # mutating x also changes its alias y    list(x = x[], alias_y = y[]) }) #> $x #> [1] 22 38 22 38 22 22 #>  #> $alias_y #> [1] 22 38 22 38 22 22

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: matrix-product kernels dispatch dgemm selectable backend. default R’s BLAS (user-swappable via update-alternatives, LD_PRELOAD, FlexiBLAS). Downstream packages register alternative (e.g. GPU --core-aware GEMM) Rfmalloc_register_matmul_backend C-callable; selection Rfmalloc-scoped, base R’s %*% unaffected. backend can also register codec-aware hook (Rfmalloc_register_matmul_backend_ex) receives compressed tensor’s raw codec payload multiplies dense operand without Rfmalloc decoding f64 first — quantized/device engine can multiply compressed data natively (fmalloc-mmap’d q4_k payload byte-compatible ggml Q4_K tensor). typed backend may decline codec, case Rfmalloc falls back panel-decode path. Together codec backend registries make stack pluggable tiers — bytes decode hardware multiplies.
code: fmalloc_matmul_backend()   # "blas" by default #> [1] "blas" fmalloc_matmul_backends()  # names registered by other packages (none here) #> character(0)

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: destroy_fmalloc_vector() immediately releases native bookkeeping one fmalloc ALTREP vector returns logical indicating whether live vector destroyed. helper enforces parent-reference safety: vector destroyed still stored child fmalloc list. needed, drop parent links first. persistent mode, destroying default semantics retains payload bytes recorded -disk allocation remains recoverable normal serialize() flows. Use destroy_fmalloc_vector(x, unsafe = TRUE) reclaim persistent payload memory mark catalog entry non-recoverable. scoped targeted vector(s): one object unsafe-destroyed, objects runtime remain recoverable.
code: local({   destroy_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(destroy_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(destroy_file)   }, add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    parent <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   parent[[1]] <- child    destroy_error <- try(destroy_fmalloc_vector(child), silent = TRUE)    parent[[1]] <- NULL   destroy_ok <- destroy_fmalloc_vector(child)    list(     destroy_rejected_with_parent = inherits(destroy_error, "try-error"),     destroy_succeeded_after_unset = destroy_ok   ) }) #> $destroy_rejected_with_parent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $destroy_succeeded_after_unset #> [1] TRUE local({   selective_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(selective_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(selective_file)   }, add = TRUE)    keep <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   drop <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   keep[] <- c(101L, 202L, 303L)   drop[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    keep_blob <- serialize(keep, NULL)   drop_blob <- serialize(drop, NULL)    destroy_fmalloc_vector(drop, unsafe = TRUE)    keep_recovered <- unserialize(keep_blob)   drop_recover_error <- try(unserialize(drop_blob), silent = TRUE)    list(     keep_recovered_ok = all.equal(keep_recovered[], c(101L, 202L, 303L)) == TRUE,     drop_recover_fails = inherits(drop_recover_error, "try-error")   ) }) #> $keep_recovered_ok #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $drop_recover_fails #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: open_fmalloc() init_fmalloc() accept mode = "persistent" mode = "scratch". default "persistent". persistent mode, committed vector payloads returned fmalloc vector finalizers. Fixed-width atomic vectors serialize reference physical allocation backing file. scratch mode, backing file temporary allocation arena. Vector finalizers may return payloads fmalloc, serialization falls back ordinary R values.
code: local({   persist_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(persist_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(persist_file)   }, add = TRUE)    x <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 5, runtime = rt)   x[] <- 1:5   roundtrip <- unserialize(serialize(x, NULL))   roundtrip[] }) #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: R’s internal inspector dispatches ALTREP Inspect hook. Rfmalloc’s inspector reports vector type, length, payload byte count, runtime mode, runtime state, payload offset, file UUID, backing-file path. inspect() output internal R diagnostic, exact formatting can vary R versions. fmalloc_altrep ... line comes Rfmalloc’s ALTREP Inspect method.
code: local({   inspect_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(inspect_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(inspect_file)   }, add = TRUE)    inspect_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   inspect_vec[] <- 1:4   .Internal(inspect(inspect_vec)) }) #> @61b0e667a468 13 INTSXP g0c0 [OBJ,REF(1),ATT] fmalloc_altrep type=integer length=4 bytes=16 data=0x7f1b0d4023e8 mode=persistent runtime=open offset=9192 uuid=2e66609e226ea0edbc65a25c831e8d37 file=/tmp/Rtmp3rxYYx/file155444f317dea.bin #> ATTRIB: #>   @61b0e66794a8 02 LISTSXP g0c0 [REF(1)]  #>     TAG: @61b0e20c7e40 01 SYMSXP g1c0 [MARK,REF(38399),LCK,gp=0x6000] "class" (has value) #>     @61b0e7224e98 16 STRSXP g0c3 [REF(65535)] (len=3, tl=0) #>       @61b0e75f28c8 09 CHARSXP g0c2 [MARK,REF(58),gp=0x60] [ASCII] [cached] "fmalloc_vector" #>       @61b0e6fa2cc0 09 CHARSXP g0c1 [MARK,REF(210),gp=0x60] [ASCII] [cached] "fmalloc" #>       @61b0e20fadf8 09 CHARSXP g1c1 [MARK,REF(623),gp=0x61] [ASCII] [cached] "integer"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc character vectors ALTSTRING vectors. String bytes, lengths, encodings, NA flags live fmalloc storage. R CHARSXP values materialized STRING_ELT() access; Rfmalloc allocate CHARSXP objects inside fmalloc.
code: local({   char_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(char_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(char_file)   }, add = TRUE)    chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("one", NA_character_, "three")    from_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3, runtime = rt)   from_int[] <- 1:3    list(     chars = chars[],     from_integer = as.character(from_int)[]   ) }) #> $chars #> [1] "one"   NA      "three" #>  #> $from_integer #> [1] "1" "2" "3"

what: Matrix and data.frame constructors and converters
title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Use constructor helpers shape-aware allocation, as_fmalloc_*() helpers install shape metadata existing fmalloc-backed vectors:
code: local({   ctor_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ctor_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(ctor_file)   }, add = TRUE)    m <- create_fmalloc_matrix("integer", nrow = 2L, ncol = 3L, runtime = rt)   m[] <- 1:6    base_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 6L, runtime = rt)   base_vec[] <- 11:16   m_view <- as_fmalloc_matrix(base_vec, ncol = 3L, copy = FALSE)    # Metadata-only reshape, then mutate through the reshaped object   m_view[1L, 2L] <- 88L    a <- create_fmalloc_array("numeric", dim = c(2L, 1L, 3L), runtime = rt)   a[] <- 1:6    col_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3L, runtime = rt)   col_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 3L, runtime = rt)   col_a[] <- c(1L, 2L, 3L)   col_b[] <- c(4L, 5L, 6L)   df <- as_fmalloc_data_frame(a = col_a, b = col_b, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    list(     matrix_dims = dim(m),     metadata_shares_payload = c(base_vec[3L], m_view[1L, 2L]),     array_dims = dim(a),     df_columns = names(df)   ) }) #> $matrix_dims #> [1] 2 3 #>  #> $metadata_shares_payload #> [1] 13 88 #>  #> $array_dims #> [1] 2 1 3 #>  #> $df_columns #> [1] "a" "b"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: rowSums(), colSums(), rowMeans(), colMeans(), Summary pathways keep small results regular R objects default avoid unnecessary small temporary mappings. can tune behavior : options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = n) n maximum allowed result length keep -memory. Results length greater n stay fmalloc-backed.
code: local({   reduce_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(reduce_file, mode = "persistent")   old_limit <- getOption("Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = old_limit)     unlink(reduce_file)   }, add = TRUE)    m <- create_fmalloc_matrix("integer", nrow = 4L, ncol = 4L, runtime = rt)   m[] <- 1:16    base_m <- matrix(1:16, nrow = 4L, ncol = 4L)   tiny_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4L, runtime = rt)   tiny_vec[] <- 11:14    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 5L)   default_row_sums <- rowSums(m)   default_range <- range(tiny_vec)    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 3L)   compact_row_sums <- rowSums(m)    options(Rfmalloc.reduce_result_length = 1L)   compact_range <- range(tiny_vec)    list(     default_row_sums_in_memory = inherits(default_row_sums, "fmalloc"),     compact_row_sums_filebacked = inherits(compact_row_sums, "fmalloc"),     default_range_in_memory = inherits(default_range, "fmalloc"),     compact_range_filebacked = inherits(compact_range, "fmalloc"),     rowSums_values = unclass(compact_row_sums),     expected_rowSums = rowSums(base_m)   ) }) #> $default_row_sums_in_memory #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $compact_row_sums_filebacked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $default_range_in_memory #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $compact_range_filebacked #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $rowSums_values #> [1] 28 32 36 40 #>  #> $expected_rowSums #> [1] 28 32 36 40

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Runtime handles make possible use one backing file one R process. Calling cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a) marks rt_a closed. vectors rt_a still reachable, native mapping kept alive vectors garbage-collected. implemented native live-vector count ALTREP-held external pointer fmalloc-backed vector; user-visible attribute added vector.
code: local({   file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(file_a)   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(file_b, size_gb = 0.1)    vec_a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 10, runtime = rt_a)   vec_b <- create_fmalloc_vector("numeric", 10, runtime = rt_b)   vec_a[1] <- 101L   vec_b[1] <- 202    before_cleanup <- data.frame(     vector = c("vec_a", "vec_b"),     value = c(vec_a[1], vec_b[1])   )    cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)   after_cleanup <- vec_a[1]   cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)   unlink(c(file_a, file_b))    list(     before_cleanup = before_cleanup,     vec_a_after_cleanup = after_cleanup   ) }) #> $before_cleanup #>   vector value #> 1  vec_a   101 #> 2  vec_b   202 #>  #> $vec_a_after_cleanup #> [1] 101

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc ALTREP serialization path persistent runtimes. persistent fixed-width atomic character vectors, serialize() stores small reference record instead copying vector payload serialization stream. record contains: format tag: "RfmallocRef"; reference format version; backing-file path; backing-file UUID; R vector type; vector length; payload offset inside mapped file; payload byte size. unserialize(), Rfmalloc reopens backing file, verifies UUID, checks recorded dimensions file bounds, validates catalog record generation, reconstructs ALTREP vector around fmalloc allocation.
code: local({   ser_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(ser_file, mode = "persistent")    ints <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   ints[] <- 101:104   chars <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 3, runtime = rt)   chars[] <- c("alpha", NA_character_, "gamma")    catalog <- list_fmalloc_allocations(rt)   ints_blob <- serialize(ints, NULL)   chars_blob <- serialize(chars, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    ints_recovered <- unserialize(ints_blob)   chars_recovered <- unserialize(chars_blob)   output <- list(     catalog = catalog[, c("record_offset", "generation", "type", "length")],     integers = ints_recovered[],     characters = chars_recovered[]   )    unlink(ser_file)   output }) #> $catalog #>   record_offset generation      type length #> 1          9440          2 character      3 #> 2          9224          1   integer      4 #>  #> $integers #> [1] 101 102 103 104 #>  #> $characters #> [1] "alpha" NA      "gamma"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Use diagnose_fmalloc_runtime() inspect runtime metadata, live catalog, compact summary can help decide runtime reset candidate: Scratch runtimes use ordinary R serialization instead. serialized values depend reopening scratch backing file. Rfmalloc list vectors intentionally general R lists. Element assignment accepts NULL Rfmalloc-backed vectors runtime; ordinary R objects base vectors, data frames, arbitrary lists rejected. keeps lists inside file-backed object universe. persistent runtimes, list containers now serialize reference state children recoverable fmalloc vectors. serialized state stores per-slot child descriptors, nested list containers can reopen recursively backing file without carrying session-local SEXP pointers. reference-based recovery, name-based object discovery. catalog stores physical allocation records, user variable names. Serialized references use catalog record offset generation validation; catalog can listed, yet high-level object store recovering vectors name.
code: local({   diag_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(diag_file, mode = "persistent")    a <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = rt)   b <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   a[] <- 1:4   b[] <- c("left", "right")    pre <- diagnose_fmalloc_runtime(rt)   destroy_fmalloc_vector(a)   post <- diagnose_fmalloc_runtime(rt)    list(     mode = post$runtime$mode,     pre_records = pre$summary$record_count,     pre_committed = pre$summary$committed_records,     post_records = post$summary$record_count,     post_tombstones = post$summary$tombstoned_records,     compaction_implemented = post$summary$compaction_implemented   ) }) #> $mode #> [1] "persistent" #>  #> $pre_records #> [1] 2 #>  #> $pre_committed #> [1] 2 #>  #> $post_records #> [1] 2 #>  #> $post_tombstones #> [1] 1 #>  #> $compaction_implemented #> [1] FALSE local({   scratch_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   scratch_rt <- open_fmalloc(scratch_file, mode = "scratch")    scratch_vec <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 4, runtime = scratch_rt)   scratch_vec[] <- 1:4   scratch_copy <- unserialize(serialize(scratch_vec, NULL))    cleanup_fmalloc(scratch_rt)   unlink(scratch_file)   scratch_copy }) #> [1] 1 2 3 4 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fmalloc_vector" "fmalloc"        "integer"

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: List assignment rejects non-fmalloc payloads runtime. Nested fmalloc lists recover recursively serialized unserialized persistent backing file. Cross-runtime insertion also rejected.
code: local({   reject_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(reject_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(reject_file)   }, add = TRUE)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- c(1L, 2L)   fm_list[[1]] <- child    bad_assignment <- try({     fm_list[[2]] <- 1:2   }, silent = TRUE)    list(     same_runtime_child = fm_list[[1]][],     rejected_non_fmalloc_payload = inherits(bad_assignment, "try-error")   ) }) #> $same_runtime_child #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $rejected_non_fmalloc_payload #> [1] TRUE local({   recover_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- open_fmalloc(recover_file, mode = "persistent")   on.exit(unlink(recover_file), add = TRUE)    child <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt)   child[] <- 1:2    nested <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt)   nested[[1]] <- child    labels <- create_fmalloc_vector("character", 2, runtime = rt)   labels[] <- c("alpha", "beta")    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 2, runtime = rt)   fm_list[[1]] <- nested   fm_list[[2]] <- labels    blob <- serialize(fm_list, NULL)    cleanup_fmalloc(rt)    recovered <- unserialize(blob)   list(     recovered_nested = recovered[[1]][[1]][],     recovered_labels = recovered[[2]]   ) }) #> $recovered_nested #> [1] 1 2 #>  #> $recovered_labels #> [1] "alpha" "beta" local({   cross_file_a <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   cross_file_b <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")    rt_a <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_a, mode = "persistent")   rt_b <- open_fmalloc(cross_file_b, mode = "persistent")   on.exit({     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_a)     cleanup_fmalloc(rt_b)     unlink(c(cross_file_a, cross_file_b))   }, add = TRUE)    child_other_runtime <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", 2, runtime = rt_b)   child_other_runtime[] <- c(10L, 20L)    fm_list <- create_fmalloc_vector("list", 1, runtime = rt_a)   cross_runtime_error <- try({     fm_list[[1]] <- child_other_runtime   }, silent = TRUE)    list(cross_runtime_rejected = inherits(cross_runtime_error, "try-error")) }) #> $cross_runtime_rejected #> [1] TRUE

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc vectors regular R vectors R’s point view, different operations exercise different paths R ALTREP: vectorized sequential operations sum(x) can use contiguous payload access; scalar loops use ALTREP element access call-heavy; x[] creates fmalloc-backed subset copy supported vector types; bench::mark() reports R heap allocation mem_alloc; count bytes stored fmalloc mapped file. small benchmark uses bench scratch runtime, benchmark-fmalloc allocations preserved persistent records. Timings machine- R-version-specific; use template local measurements.
code: perf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin") rt <- open_fmalloc(perf_file, mode = "scratch", size_gb = 0.1)  n <- 100000L set.seed(1) idx <- sample.int(n, 2000L)  base_int <- integer(n) base_int[] <- seq_len(n)  fm_int <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) fm_int[] <- base_int  write_env <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) write_env$base <- base_int write_env$fm <- create_fmalloc_vector("integer", n, runtime = rt) write_env$fm[] <- base_int  scalar_sum <- function(x, i) {   total <- 0L   for (j in i) total <- total + x[[j]]   total }  perf_result <- bench::mark(   base_sequential_sum = sum(base_int),   fmalloc_sequential_sum = sum(fm_int),   base_scalar_read = scalar_sum(base_int, idx),   fmalloc_scalar_read = scalar_sum(fm_int, idx),   base_subset_copy = base_int[idx],   fmalloc_subset_copy = fm_int[idx],   base_indexed_write = {     write_env$base[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$base[1L])   },   fmalloc_indexed_write = {     write_env$fm[idx] <- 0L     invisible(write_env$fm[1L])   },   iterations = 20,   check = FALSE )[, c("expression", "median", "itr/sec", "mem_alloc", "gc/sec")]  rm(fm_int, write_env) invisible(gc()) cleanup_fmalloc(rt) unlink(perf_file) perf_result #> # A tibble: 8 × 5 #>   expression               median `itr/sec` mem_alloc `gc/sec` #>   <bch:expr>             <bch:tm>     <dbl> <bch:byt>    <dbl> #> 1 base_sequential_sum     38.74µs    25035.        0B        0 #> 2 fmalloc_sequential_sum  47.35µs    20462.        0B        0 #> 3 base_scalar_read        36.99µs    24770.        0B        0 #> 4 fmalloc_scalar_read      1.02ms      953.   24.55KB        0 #> 5 base_subset_copy         4.82µs   189354.    7.86KB        0 #> 6 fmalloc_subset_copy     21.93µs    39509.        0B        0 #> 7 base_indexed_write      27.04µs    35321.  390.67KB        0 #> 8 fmalloc_indexed_write  206.74µs     4782.        0B        0

what: Native C API for Other Packages
title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Rfmalloc installs inst/include/Rfmalloc.h registers C-callable entry points R_RegisterCCallable(). Downstream packages can add Rfmalloc LinkingTo Imports, include header, use inline wrappers. current native surface (API version 5) exposes runtime open/cleanup, vector creation, default-runtime lookup synchronization, catalog listing, runtime/vector introspection, vector destruction, API-version query, two extension registries: Rfmalloc_register_tensor_codec (plug element encoding panel-streamed matrix products — Rgguf uses quantized GGUF formats) Rfmalloc_register_matmul_backend (plug GEMM kernel behind matrix products). Returned SEXP objects follow normal R API ownership rules.

title: Persistent ALTREP Vectors
text: Storage / allocator: fmalloc: https://github.com/yasukata/fmalloc Simon Urbanek’s Rf_allocVector3() custom mmap allocator PoC: https://gist.github.com/s-u/6712c97ca74181f5a1a5 Rfmalloc’s prior custom allocator implementation using Rf_allocVector3() (historical comparison): https://github.com/sounkou-bioinfo/Rfmalloc/commit/0165953 Compression: ALP (Adaptive Lossless floating-Point Compression), Afroozeh, Kuffo & Boncz, https://doi.org/10.1145/3626717; reference implementation https://github.com/cwida/ALP. Rfmalloc’s "alp" codec adapts scalar core Mike Cheng’s MIT-licensed zap. -place / -reference mutation: insitu (modify R vectors reference, bypassing copy--modify): https://github.com/coolbutuseless/insitu Related work (memory-mapped / larger--RAM matrices R): houba — memory-mapped vectors/matrices/arrays: https://github.com/HervePerdry/houba bigstatsr / bigsnpr (FBM): https://privefl.github.io/bigstatsr/, https://privefl.github.io/bigsnpr/ bigmemory: https://cran.r-project.org/package=bigmemory vroom (lazy file index): https://vroom.r-lib.org

title: Rfmalloc: Memory-Mapped File Allocation for R — Rfmalloc-package
text: ALTREP-backed dispatch now covers core Ops, Summary, Math, Math2, matrix rowSums/colSums/rowMeans/colMeans, initial matrixOps workflows S3 methods common vector/matrix usage. Explicit base-fallback boundaries : rowSums(), colSums(), rowMeans(), colMeans() input exact 2D matrix dims != 1L; cases now emit warning call corresponding base:: reducer. Scalar zero-length results Summary, Math, Math2 generics (example sum(x) returning single value) returned ordinary R scalars design. Matrix products use BLAS dgemm finite numeric operands; operands containing NA/NaN/Inf logical/integer/complex operands computed managed native loops base-consistent semantics. Full operator- method-family coverage still incomplete R generics. advanced families may still materialize ordinary R objects edge cases.
